comments.text: Coldest air on Earth today is in the US with temps up to 40 degrees below normal in the Midwest/ Great Lakes. Actual lows are as low as -20F there. Cold US is due to widespread relative warmth in the Arctic, with record low Arctic Sea Ice extent. This “warmth” displaces the polar cold pool south.

I keep thinking about that Bill Nye "we'll have to farm Canada" thing and how so many of us still think about our personal (or regional) climate change adaptation as being "move north" . Well, this is the weather forecast for tomorrow... There's very little north that isn't also just as hot.

The season’s first snows have frosted the Rocky Mountains, northern Plains, Great Lakes and northern New England over the past week, resulting in the most extensive early-November snow cover in at least two decades.

It’s been the coldest winter in 15 years for parts of the Northeast and Mid-Atlantic, with record ice cover on the Great Lakes and rare snow in the tropics. Meanwhile, the West has seen unusually warm temperatures.

It may be cold today for many🥶, but winter as a season is warming quickly across the country🌡️📈. Learn more about how winter is changing in the long-term across the U.S. tomorrow at our new climate-focused webinar series! 🗓️Tuesday, December 16 | 12pm ET
🔗Register: buff.ly/HOGFZIP

A storm could bring snow, sleet or freezing rain to about 55 percent of the contiguous U.S. this weekend. Temperatures will dip lower than minus-30, as a polar vortex sends dangerously cold air into the Midwest, Great Lakes and New England.

We were under a winter storm warning for today here on the northwest corner of Lake Michigan and we haven't gotten a stitch of snow but we have gotten over an inch of rain and it is just coating everything here. It's freezing instantly. #906Life #mymichigan

Instead of complaining about winter, I am reminding myself that The Sun will be back. She won’t be gone forever. We’re biding our time. Our strength will be renewed!! Oh, to be a gay homosexual at the beach. *sigh*

Climate change has shrunk ice coverage, on average, year-to-year on Lake Superior, making ice-cave access anomalous. Eleven years ago, in 2015, I couldn’t make it to the caves, and I’ve had FOMO ever since.

The formation of 'Ice Flowers' is a natural phenomenon in the Great Lakes Region of North America When the temperature drops under freezing temperatures, the water under the ice layer is pushed upward, creating small cracks. Cont⬇️

The forecast for much of the USA calls for colder temperatures and frozen water, but look at the trends. Meteorological winter (December - February) 4-7F. milder than they were in 1970 from the Upper Midwest and Great Lakes into New England. #wintershrinkage Map courtesy of Climate Central, Axios

Who says climate change isn't real? It was 42°F and sunny, and no ice or snow here today in Sturgeon Bay, Wisconsin on the 30th of December. I took these pix on my beach walk at Whitefish Bay State Park here on the Door Pennisula, along Lake Michigan.

Some fish species also use the ice for protection from predators during spawning season which relies on ice cover.. that cannot happen if the water is too warm #climate #cdnpoli #onpoli #LakeMichigan #GreenSky
If y'all think mitigating climate change is going to be expensive, just wait until you see how much "doing nothing" is going to cost. (Great Lakes without winter ice.)

Polar vortex expected to bring coldest temperatures this winter to Toronto On Sunday, a polar vortex is set to bring the coldest temperatures the city has had this winter, with temperatures feeling like as low as - 25 C near the Great Lakes.
NWS Climate Prediction Center coming in ho… uh, cold. real, real fuckin cold

Great Lakes ice is thought to block evaporation. A new study says weather plays a bigger role. Study challenges assumption that ice cover leads to reduced evaporation #GreatLakes www.wpr.org/news/great-l... agupubs.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10....

Look, I do love winter, and having a proper replenishing snow pack- However! We could maybe spread it out a little more? And perhaps not always have it on my work nights? Hopefully, we’ll be clear enough for Saturday’s Christmas Bird Count! 😅 #winter #PAwx #LakeEffect

In the photo on the right, there are blocks of peat ripped up from an outcrop offshore. The peat predates the first ice advance into this area during the last glaciation (but I can't recall the 14C age someone got from the outcrop🤷‍♂️)
Natural-gas prices have jumped this week in response to forecasts calling for some of the coldest, snowiest weather in years to freeze the country from the West Texas desert to the Great Lakes.

Climate scientists are predicting ice storms in Ontario will increase in number and severity, especially in the Great lakes region. It's time to start taking climate change seriously and start doing something about it.
People who love this mild weather don't realize what a disaster it can be for winter tourism, the Great Lakes ecosystem, agriculture, etc. I would happily dress warmer to have a real winter back.

December Rewind: ♨️ 12 U.S. cities had their hottest December on record
🌡️ 95 of 194 cities were warmer than average
📍 The Southwest was most unusual: temps ran 9.4°F+ above normal
🥵 Casper, WY was warmest, ending 12.1°F above average Details + Data 👉 www.climatecentral.org/report/month...

It was also the driest March on record in California. Precipitation was also much below average in the Rocky Mountains, Desert Southwest and Southeast. Only the Pacific Northwest and Great Lakes region were wetter than normal.

The lack of a killing freeze in most of the SE US following multiple late-season tropical remnants has led to a greener landscape compared to this time last year. Fall fire season impacts may be delayed as a result, while drought-and frost-cured fuels are readily burning in the NE. shorturl.at/nC0uX

Below zero readings made it all the way to the Illinois border this morning, with a -3°F reading at Walworth. Wind chills fell into the -20s in places like Arlington, Kewaskum, Grantsburg, Spooner, Crandon, and Manitowish Waters. This is the coldest weather we've seen since the middle of February.

A wave-break in the Atlantic will force a retrograde of the Greenland High, setting up a decent late fall cold air outbreak to descend from central Canada into the Great Lakes and much of the Eastern US. Meanwhile, much of mid- and high-latitude Eurasia will be extremely warm over the same period.

I'm with you here. I can always put on another layer. There's a limit to how many I can remove. I've lived in three climate zones in the US: the Great Lakes region, southern CA, and the SF Bay area. Weather wise, I actually liked Los Angeles the least, and find southern FL unpleasant even in winter

It's becoming more difficult for our weather people to predict the weather because of climate change, and more ice storms is a result of a changing climate. It's 60° at the southern end of Lake Michigan and 33° at the top end.

A brief spring chill is expected on Monday accompanied by snow showers and breezy north winds. More snowflakes may fly on Wednesday, as well. Cool temperatures will continue through the middle of the week before gradually rising toward the end of the week. #ILwx #INwx

Ice cover is WAY below average on the Great Lakes. The lake ice season acroos the N hemisphere is getting shorter because of climate change. The ice season is even shorter on the Great Lakes during El Nino winters, which tend to start mild.
Winter Bear Wear is a #handmade #scarf #toque collection I created to keep me warm while I live on the shore of Great Lake Ontario in my tiny Canadian village. Order yours today. #follow

How can climate change mean more snow? The Great Lakes region is experiencing it this winter. Warmer than normal lake water means less ice cover. Cold arctic winds over warmer open lake water means more lake effect snow.

I really thought ice cover would have had a substantial effect on evaporation from Lake Superior in winter. It would have some effect from first principles but apparently it is small. Here is a pic of steam fog evaporating from open water but impeded by ice during very cold winter conditions.
Please tell me more how climate change isn't real

#FantasyIndiesApril 02: Seasons Since the story takes place on Earth, the seasons are more or less the same. By 2036 climate change has really snowballed, which is why Ruby could hang out in the middle of Lake Ontario in a jacket on New Years Eve. In Toronto, winter is scant and summer is long.
Ice coverage rests on the waters of the Great Lakes in various bays, inlets and along their coastlines while snow peppers the surrounding land, observed this afternoon by NOAA-21.
When the ice on Toronto Harbour starts to break up in late winter, Polar Bears who ventured onto the ice sheet to hunt seals start coming back to shore.

I'm not sure where you are in the world, but that water is beautiful. I live near Lake Michigan and we are icy....very icy right now, and there's a 4 foot snowdrift in front of my garage door. But, it's really pretty after a snow.

Winter in the northern hemisphere sees the growth of ice cover over bodies of water. In the Great Lakes, rounds of frigid Arctic air infiltrating the contiguous United States allowed ice cover to develop along the coast, in bays and inlets, and over Lake Erie.

Filing a complaint with the sources of climate change this +11C February day (!!!) and dropping air pressure has completely wiped me out.
I am so dizzy and woozy.
And this low is moving FAST. (and it's going to get sharply colder really fast.)

Cloud coverage increases in the winter normally but with the changing climate and warmer water temperatures, it is accelerating evaporation on Lake Superior and Lake Michigan which in turn increases our cloud coverage.

• Educate.Innovate.Agitate.

I'm not sure if I've seen it happening. Rime ice events in Toronto are often associated with temperatures between -4°C and -7°C and the presence of fog banks. The proximity to the Great Lakes can influence moisture levels, but the specific combination of fog and sub-zero temperatures is needed.

Funding for the NOAA Office of Oceanic and Atmospheric Research, including climate research, is preserved at a small bump over FY25 levels, instead of being zeroed out. The Trump plan to transfer weather programs to NWS is accepted.

Aomori City, in Japan, gets an average of 312 inches of snow (26 feet of snow) each year. It's the snowiest city on the planet. In the United States, the snowiest city in the lower 48 states is Syracuse, which gets an average of 11 feet of snow each year. (Lake effect snow, from Lake Ontario.).

I live in the midwest Great Lakes area. I know i should be use to snow round this time ofbyear but sometimes....just sometimes I would like it to just snow mid december. I just i do not look forward to what winter weather is going to happen this year given how warm it was through Oct.
